steps.title: Identify the appositive structure
steps.content: The blank contains two noun elements: “linguist” (a common noun) and “Diane Massam” (a proper name). The name immediately follows the title, creating an appositive—a grammatical structure where one noun renames or specifies another.

steps.title: Apply the restrictive appositive rule
steps.content: A restrictive appositive takes no comma. Since “Diane Massam” is essential to identifying which linguist (it specifies a particular person), it's restrictive, so no punctuation should separate it from “linguist.” Options A and B violate this by adding commas; option D adds a colon, which is inappropriate. That matches choice C — C is correct.
